message_text: I want to give an update about my remaining loan payment. I will not be able to pay this month.

I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira, and this conversation is being logged. I’m contacting you about your Nira loan ending ****4831. To verify I’m speaking with the right person, please confirm the last four digits of the account reference.
I’m not confirming anything again—I already told you I can’t pay right now, and I’m out of work.

Understood. You’ve said you can’t pay right now and you’re out of work. I’m closing the assessment here and passing this forward for hardship review. Please note your current amount due is 12921 on the Nira loan ending ****4831.

{"from_stage": "ASSESSMENT", "to_stage": "RESOLUTION", "summary": "Disclosures completed earlier in this conversation. Debt context was established with the borrower earlier: Nira loan ending ****4831; amount due 12921 was not previously stated in transcript and was stated this turn. Transcript-supported identity verification is not visible in this conversation; case state indicates identity_verified=true, but the borrower refused to confirm again and said they are not confirming anything again. Borrower stated they cannot pay right now/this month and are out of work, which is a hardship signal (job loss). Borrower also gave a boundary signal by saying they already told us they cannot pay right now. Financial facts learned/confirmed: available now 0; no payment this month; case state shows prior can_pay_later around 2026-04-20, but borrower did not reaffirm that in this turn. Objection/refusal: refused repeat verification; stop-contact flag exists in case state, so avoid further outreach inconsistent with that flag. What was already asked: verification via last four digits was requested once. Priority for Agent 2: address hardship review first and avoid repeating verification or capacity questions already answered in this channel; if policy permits, review policy-allowed options in next_allowed_actions (OFFER_REDUCED_CLOSURE, OFFER_PAYMENT_PLAN) with hardship sensitivity, and avoid further contact if the stop-contact flag controls outreach."}

I’ve already told you—I’m unemployed and I’m not promising any payment right now.

Understood. I’ve recorded that you’re unemployed, cannot pay right now, and are not making any payment commitment at this time. Nira’s current policy allows only approval-based options, and any payment arrangement must complete within 30 days; based on what you’ve stated, I’m not asking for a payment commitment now. Because hardship is present, the next policy-compliant step is hardship review. I’ve also noted the existing no-contact status, so no further outreach should occur inconsistent with that flag.

{"from_stage": "RESOLUTION", "to_stage": "FINAL_NOTICE", "summary": "Conversation already in progress; identity disclosure and recording disclosure were already completed earlier. Borrower\u2019s latest position is that they are unemployed, cannot pay right now or this month, and will not make any payment commitment at this time; they also previously refused repeat verification. Hardship signals include job loss, with prior case flags also showing medical issue/student. Agent 2 checked available loan data and Nira policy: only approval-based reduced-closure and payment-plan paths are allowed, and any arrangement must complete within 30 days. No concrete option was presented because the borrower stated zero current capacity and no commitment. Stop-contact flag already exists in case state; Agent 2 told the borrower their hardship status and no-payment position were recorded, that the next policy-compliant step is hardship review, and that no outreach should occur inconsistent with the stop-contact flag. Agent 3 should continue naturally without restarting, avoid repeating disclosures, verification, or generic payment questions, and avoid any outreach unless a documented permitted next step exists that is consistent with the existing stop-contact status."}
